articles.title: Everything you need to know about Zach Bryan’s planned Jack Kerouac Center in Mass.
articles.description:
A historic Lowell church tied to Jack Kerouac’s life could soon become a performance venue, museum, and cultural hub.

The post Everything you need to know about Zach Bryan’s planned Jack Kerouac Center in Mass. appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Zach Bryan plans to transform a historic Lowell church into a cultural hub.
The center will honor Jack Kerouac's legacy through performances and exhibits.
Local residents may benefit from increased tourism and cultural activities in Lowell.

articles.title: Army reviewing after helicopters hovered alongside Kid Rock’s swimming pool as he saluted
articles.description:
Kid Rock posted two videos on social media on Saturday. Each shows a helicopter hovering alongside his swimming pool.

The post Army reviewing after helicopters hovered alongside Kid Rock’s swimming pool as he saluted appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Army reviewing incident of helicopters hovering near Kid Rock's pool on March 31.
Kid Rock shared two videos of the helicopters on social media.
Local residents concerned about military activities near private properties.

articles.title: Gas prices eclipse $4 a gallon in the U.S., the highest since 2022
articles.description:
According to motor club AAA, the national average for a gallon of regular gasoline is now $4.02 — over a dollar more than before the war began.

The post Gas prices eclipse $4 a gallon in the U.S., the highest since 2022 appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Gas prices in Boston reach $4.02 per gallon, highest since 2022.
Prices have increased over a dollar since the war began.
Rising fuel costs may strain local budgets for Boston residents.

articles.title: How one family’s bipolar disorder experience led to more than $1 billion for the Broad Institute in Cambridge
articles.description:
The Broad Institute launched in 2004 to tackle disease research with the combined forces of faculty from MIT, Harvard, and other scientists.

The post How one family’s bipolar disorder experience led to more than $1 billion for the Broad Institute in Cambridge appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Broad Institute in Cambridge raised over $1 billion for disease research since its 2004 launch.
Funding was influenced by a family's personal experience with bipolar disorder.
Boston residents benefit from advancements in mental health research and treatments.

articles.title: Brookline runner takes on first Boston Marathon for One World Strong
articles.description:
Elena Castellini is raising money for a nonprofit founded by a 2013 bombing survivor to help communities heal after trauma.

The post Brookline runner takes on first Boston Marathon for One World Strong appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Elena Castellini runs her first Boston Marathon on April 17, 2023.
She raises funds for One World Strong, a nonprofit aiding trauma recovery.
The organization was founded by a survivor of the 2013 Boston Marathon bombing.

articles.title: Home of the Week: Converted church in Nahant with ocean views, in-law apartment for $1.6m
articles.description:
The 1851 church is now a six-bedroom house with a roof deck and sunroom

The post Home of the Week: Converted church in Nahant with ocean views, in-law apartment for $1.6m appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Converted 1851 church in Nahant listed for $1.6 million.
Property features six bedrooms, a roof deck, and an in-law apartment.
Offers ocean views, appealing to Boston residents seeking unique homes.
